provenance.rationale: Round_02 produced a deterministic monotone signal on rerank_in: 10 trials at rerank_in=16 ALL scored mean_mrr_at_10=0.6717; 4 trials at rerank_in=24 ALL scored 0.6673; 2 trials at rerank_in=32 ALL scored 0.664. The bundle's param_importances put rerank_in at 0.9827 vs every other axis below 0.006 — rerank_in is dominating the signal so completely that no other axis is readable. Narrowing rerank_in to {16} is anti-pattern A11 evidence: bundle.statistics.boundary_hits and the trial values prove the {24, 32} choices are strictly worse, and the extreme determinism (every trial in each bucket has identical value to 4 decimal places) means we are not throwing away exploration room — we are removing a settled axis. Once fixed at 16, round_03 should expose whether mmr_lambda / title_cap_* / final_top_k / mmr_k carry any signal that was masked. mmr_lambda kept at [0.55, 0.75] because statistics.axis_coverage[mmr_lambda].note still reports both edges UNSAMPLED (sampled range 0.554-0.746 in round_02 trials); A10 forbids narrowing on UNSAMPLED edges. seed bumped 43→44 to avoid replaying round_02's exact param sequence after the rerank_in narrowing.

diff_summary.evidence: param_importances.rerank_in=0.9827; deterministic buckets in bundle.trials: rerank_in=16 → mrr=0.6717 (10/10), rerank_in=24 → mrr=0.6673 (4/4), rerank_in=32 → mrr=0.664 (2/2)
reseed after a search_space narrowing so TPE samples a fresh sequence

notes: rerank_in fixed at 16. With it out of the variance budget, round_03's importance ranking should reveal which other axis (likely final_top_k or one of the title_cap_* pair) carries the next signal. mmr_lambda kept wide because its edges are still UNSAMPLED.
